chunks.text: A small electronics company was founded thirty years ago at its headquarters in a regional city.
The founder was a capable engineer whose commitment was stronger than anyone's.
In the first years, the company carried debt.
The numbers in the account were harsh, and revenue was small.
But he convinced investor after investor and gathered fund little by little.
In time, the company rose in the competitive market.
It won contract with overseas client and rode the wave of expansion.
As chairman, the founder spoke to every employee at the annual conference.
" Our strength is not strategy, but the sincerity of each person," he said.
Today, the company enjoys commercial success while never forgetting its contribution to the local community.
Transparency of administration, fairness in how employees are promoted.
The story of this small firm teaches us that business success cannot be measured by numbers alone.
